crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: winforensics-mcp (whl)
Version: 1.1.0
Description: Windows Forensics MCP Server - EVTX parsing, Registry analysis, PE analysis, API Monitor knowledge base, and remote artifact collection for DFIR

The winforensics-mcp package is a comprehensive Windows forensic analysis toolkit designed as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. It provides a robust set of tools for incident responders and forensic analysts to examine Windows systems. Key features include: 1. Artifact Parsing: Specialized parsers for EVTX (Event Logs), Registry hives (SAM, SYSTEM, SOFTWARE, etc.), Prefetch (.pf), Amcache, SRUM (resource usage), MFT (Master File Table), USN Journal, LNK files, ShellBags, and Browser History (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). 2. Advanced Analysis: Static PE analysis using pefile, network capture analysis using scapy, and packer/compiler detection via Detect It Easy (DiE). 3. Threat Intelligence: Integration with VirusTotal for hash/IP/domain reputation lookups and a built-in YARA scanner with a large library of bundled rules for detecting malware families, exploits, and hacking tools. 4. Orchestration: High-level functions to investigate binary execution, build unified forensic timelines, hunt for IOCs across all sources, and reconstruct user activity. 5. Remote Collection: Capabilities to triage and collect artifacts from remote Windows hosts via WinRM (including VSS/Shadow Copy support for locked files), SSH, and SMB. The package uses standard forensic libraries and its logic is entirely consistent with its stated purpose as a forensic utility. No evidence of malicious intent, unauthorized data exfiltration, or suspicious runtime payload downloads was observed.
